_meta.note: One-shot: composes command_start_combed -> bucket_wait (bounded) -> command_status. Returns rule-driven signals AND the exit code in one call. A quiet command (no matches) returns a bounded receipt, never an error.

response_example_degraded.recover_hint: IPC error interrupted the wait, but the job is still tracked. First confirm daemon liveness with the `health` tool, then poll command_status with this job_id for the final state and signals. Do not re-run the command.

invariants: argv is explicit and shell interpreters are daemon-denied
signals contains only rule-driven events; the probe-lifecycle exit marker is reported via state/exit_code, not as a signal
a quiet command (zero rule matches) returns a bounded receipt, never an error

complete is true iff state is terminal (exited|cancelled|failed); wait_exhausted is its negation: true when the bounded wait budget was spent while the job was still running. wait_exhausted=true means STILL RUNNING -> poll command_status for the final exit_code/signals, do not treat as finished
wait_ms is bounded (default 5000, max 60000) and max_signals is bounded (default 50, max 500)
inline rules use the typed shorthand; the minimal rule is {"pattern": "..."}

env is an ARRAY of {key,value} objects, not a map; empty/omitted inherits the daemon environment, a non-empty list is OVERLAID onto (added to or overrides) the inherited environment (merged, not replaced; PATH/SystemRoot are kept)
an env map (e.g. {"FOO":"bar"}) is rejected with a teaching error naming the {key,value} array shape
a non-array argv is rejected with a field-prefixed teaching error naming the array-of-strings shape with an example (TB-11)
wait_ms is a WALL-CLOCK budget (TC-6): total wall time is bounded by wait_ms plus at most one in-flight slice and the round-trips, never ceil(wait_ms/slice)*slice
cursor is present on EVERY result (normal, wait_exhausted, and degraded) and is authoritative for resuming the bucket via bucket_events_since/bucket_wait

degraded=true (TC-1b) means an IPC error interrupted the wait AFTER a job_id was minted: the result is success-shaped (never a bare error), carries the real job_id/bucket_id/cursor, sets complete=false and wait_exhausted=true, and reports state as the last observed value or "unknown" -- never a silent "running"
recover_hint is null on the normal path and, when degraded, instructs the agent to confirm daemon health THEN poll command_status with the job_id -- do not re-run the command
a degraded result carries receipt=null because the wait did not complete
